lights on.title: Day / night indicator
lights on.body: Shows whether F2 is in its light period, worked out from measured light (PPFD above 50 umol) rather than a wall-clock schedule, so it reflects what the plants actually see. The irrigation day runs lights-on to lights-on: P0 dryback starts at lights-on and the daily water and shot counters reset there, not at midnight. Several checks switch with it too — CO2 and PPFD are only judged against target while lights are on, and CO2 staying high in the dark raises its own warning.

updated.title: Connection and data freshness
updated.body: The dashboard polls Home Assistant every 30 seconds; a green dot with an updated time means everything on the page is live. A red dot with disconnected means you are looking at the last readings it managed to fetch — do not trust any number on the page and do not expect setpoint changes to go through until it reconnects.

switch.veg_main_pump.title: Pump panel and emergency stop
switch.veg_main_pump.body: One-tap access to the main pump, mainline and the three row valves from any view. The badge shows live pump watts whenever the pump is running, and the button pulses red on a dead-head: the pump pulling over 100 W with all three row valves shut, pushing against a closed pipe — this has damaged the F2 pump before. The Emergency stop inside disables the steering system and auto irrigation, then shuts the pump, mainline and every valve; afterwards confirm pump power reads 0 W.

to sort out.title: Persistent alert strip
to sort out.body: A summary of every open advisory, pinned to the top of every tab so nothing urgent is missed while you work elsewhere. Red means at least one urgent item (act now), amber means warnings to sort out today. Tap it to jump to the full advisory cards on Status.

Advisories — what needs you.title: Advisory cards explained
Advisories — what needs you.body: Each card is produced by a rule watching live sensors — pump dead-head, zone at its dry floor, engine heartbeat silent, hotspot, condensation risk, EC stacking toward burn and more. Urgent (red) means act now; Advisory (amber) means sort it out today. Every card states what happened, the likely cause, first checks, and a concrete next step; Fix it jumps you to the exact control, Evidence shows the raw readings behind the call, and Acknowledge hides it until the rule trips again.

Keeping an eye on.title: Watch list, not alarms
Keeping an eye on.body: Things drifting but not yet worth acting on: pore EC away from target, a shallow dryback, a zone within 6 percent of its dry floor, a mildly warm back-left corner, or zones running different phases. Nothing here needs you yet — it is the early-warning tier below the advisory cards, and items either resolve themselves or graduate into a real advisory.

headline.title: One-line room verdict
headline.body: The single most important sentence about F2 right now, ranked critical, then warning, then watch, then all-good. It is computed from the same rules as the advisories, so if it says nothing needs you, every zone is above its floor, the plant is transpiring normally and climate is in band. Use Ask AI to interrogate the live state or Read aloud for hands-busy checks.

sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_temperat.title: Room air temperature
sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_temperat.body: Live canopy-level air temperature in degrees C. The target band under the value is stage-aware — it shifts automatically with the grow stage (clone, veg, stretch, bulk, finish); in bulk flower it is 24-26 C. Out of band drives VPD off target, and warmer rooms pull the dew-point margin down at night.

sensor.f2_sensor_humidity.title: Relative humidity
sensor.f2_sensor_humidity.body: Room RH in percent, with a stage-aware target (58-65 percent in bulk flower). Too low pushes VPD high and shuts stomata; too high invites mould and shrinks the condensation margin, especially in the last hour before lights-off. RH and temperature together set VPD, so fix whichever of the pair is actually off.

sensor.f2_air_vpd.title: Air vapour pressure deficit
sensor.f2_air_vpd.body: The drying power of the room air, computed from temperature and RH, in kPa. Bulk-flower target is 1.0-1.4 kPa: below it the air is too humid to pull water through the plant, above it stomata close and the plant stops drinking. This is the room-level number; Leaf VPD next to it is what the leaf actually experiences.

sensor.f2_leaf_vpd.title: Leaf-level VPD
sensor.f2_leaf_vpd.body: VPD calculated at the leaf surface using canopy leaf temperature — the real transpiration driver, usually a bit different from air VPD because LED canopies run cooler than the air. Sweet spot is 1.0-1.3 kPa. It is only judged against target while lights are on; in the dark transpiration is minimal anyway.

sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_co2.title: CO2 concentration
sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_co2.body: Room CO2 in ppm. Daytime target in bulk flower is 1200-1450 ppm to match the high light level; at night enrichment is off and the value should fall. A reading above 1800 ppm in the dark triggers an advisory — either night venting is not moving air or the sensor has drifted high; cross-check against the back-left CO2 sensor.

sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_ppfd.title: Light intensity (PPFD)
sensor.f2_scd41_env3_dlight_poe_ppfd.body: Photosynthetic light at the canopy in umol/m2/s, with today's DLI (total daily light, mol/m2) underneath. Bulk-flower target is 850-1200 umol. PPFD persistently under target while lights are on raises a dimmer-than-expected advisory — check the LED driver, the dimming setpoint and that nothing is shading the sensor. This sensor also decides the day/night state for the whole dashboard.

sensor.crop_steering_average_vwc_all_zon.title: Average substrate moisture
sensor.crop_steering_average_vwc_all_zon.body: Mean volumetric water content across all three zones, in percent — the room-level wetness number. Healthy coco in this system typically sits in the 50-65 percent range after irrigation and slides down overnight. It hides per-zone differences, so a normal average with one zone diverging is exactly what the Zones tab is for.

sensor.crop_steering_fused_ec.title: Fused pore EC
sensor.crop_steering_fused_ec.body: The salt concentration the roots actually sit in, in mS/cm, fused from the substrate probes across zones so one noisy sensor cannot skew it. The dashboard judges it against the 6.0 generative P2 target and flags amber when it is 0.8 or more off; the sub-line shows the trend per hour. Deeper drybacks concentrate salts and push it up; bigger or more frequent shots flush and pull it down — that push-pull is the whole generative EC-stacking lever.

sensor.crop_steering_dryback_percentage.title: Dryback from peak
sensor.crop_steering_dryback_percentage.body: How many percentage points VWC has dropped from its last post-irrigation peak — always dries-back-BY, never dries-back-TO. A peak of 70 and a current 58 is a 12 percent dryback. It is judged against the live dryback target: well under target (below about 60 percent of it) means the blocks are staying wet — the classic over-watering pattern, which also slows EC stacking; far over target means the room is drying harder than intended.

Cond. margin.title: Condensation safety margin
Cond. margin.body: How far the coldest tracked surface (back-left corner or substrate, minus a safety allowance) sits above the dew point, in degrees C. Keep it above 2; below 1.5 an advisory fires because water will start condensing on leaves, walls or coils — a botrytis and GMP risk. Lift it by nudging night temperature up, cutting RH, or improving airflow over the cold spots.

sensor.f2_scd41_back_left_temperature.title: Back-left hotspot temperature
sensor.f2_scd41_back_left_temperature.body: Dedicated sensor in the back-left corner, the known hot spot of F2. The plants there dry out and stress first, so it is tracked separately from the room average. A gap of 1.5 C over room temperature earns a watch; 2.6 C or more raises an advisory — that gradient is almost always internal airflow (fan throw to that corner), not the AC setpoint, so fix the airflow before touching climate.

sensor.f2_tank_level.title: Source tank level
sensor.f2_tank_level.body: How full the irrigation feed tank is, in percent, with litres remaining when available. Green above 50 percent, amber from 50 down to 20, red below 20 with a refill-soon flag. A dry tank means missed shots at exactly the moment the engine wants to water, so treat amber as the cue to mix the next batch.

Room 3D.title: 3D facility twin
Room 3D.body: A live interactive 3D model of the facility — room states, lights, climate and per-row sensors rendered on the floor plan. Nothing loads until you tap (the model is heavy), so this card is just the door. Inside, tapping a room tile deep-links back here: substrate metrics land on Zones, climate metrics on Climate.

camera.flower2_cam_clear.title: Room camera still
camera.flower2_cam_clear.body: A snapshot from the F2 room camera, refreshed roughly every 30 seconds — a sanity check that what the numbers say matches what the canopy looks like. Use it to eyeball wilt, light operation or anything physically out of place without walking to the room. If the frame goes dim the feed is unavailable; the numbers around it are unaffected.

sensor.crop_steering_activity_log.title: Engine activity feed
sensor.crop_steering_activity_log.body: The irrigation engine's own diary: it publishes a line for every watered, blocked and phase-change event, and the newest entries appear here. This is the fastest way to confirm the system actually watered when you expected — or to see why it refused (source-water gate, daily cap, safety block). The full rolling feed lives on the entity's feed attribute; about 40 recent events are kept.

Facility floor.title: Mini facility light map
Facility floor.body: A 2x2 sketch of the four rooms — F2, Veg, F1 and Vault — with a room glowing amber when any of its lights are on. Handy for catching a photoperiod mistake at a glance, like a flower room lit during its dark period. Tap anywhere on the card to open the full 3D twin.

sensor.f2_cwsi.title: Plant stress index
sensor.f2_cwsi.body: Crop water stress index from 0 to 1, inferred from canopy and climate sensors rather than any single probe. Under 0.3 the plant is comfortable; 0.3-0.5 is mildly stressed; above 0.5 needs attention and above 0.7 is critical. Rising stress with normal substrate numbers usually points at climate (VPD, heat) rather than irrigation.

sensor.f2_transpiration_cooling.title: Transpiration cooling check
sensor.f2_transpiration_cooling.body: How much cooler the leaves are than the air, in degrees C — evaporating water cools the leaf, so this is a direct read on whether the plant is actually drinking. More than 1 C cooler is a plant moving water well; under about 0.4 C means transpiration has slowed, so check VPD, light level and the root zone. It is one of the best leading indicators in the room because it reacts before VWC or EC do.

Condensation margin.title: Dew point gauge
Condensation margin.body: The same dew-point safety check as the snapshot tile, as a gauge: the sub-line shows the current dew point, and the goal is to keep all surfaces at least 2 C above it. The marker drifting left toward zero is the room approaching the temperature where water films form on leaves and cold surfaces — the precondition for mould. Most at risk in the last hour of lights and overnight.

Pore EC ~3h.title: Three-hour EC projection
Pore EC ~3h.body: Where pore EC will be in about three hours if the measured trend holds, with the rate clamped to a believable 0.4 mS/cm per hour so a post-shot blip cannot blow up the forecast. The burn line sits about 1 mS/cm above target (around 7) — tip-burn territory. This is a soft heads-up only; the actionable warning is the separate EC-stacking advisory, which only fires when EC is already at target and climbing.

Stress flags.title: Binary stress indicators
Stress flags.body: Two yes/no flags inferred from the sensor suite: root-zone (substrate) stress and light-load stress. All clear means neither is active. If one trips, it names which system to look at first — substrate stress points to the Zones tab and irrigation, light-load stress to PPFD and leaf temperature on Climate.

Substrate · last 24h.title: Per-zone VWC rhythm
Substrate · last 24h.body: Real recorded history of each zone's substrate moisture over the last 24 hours — this is the steering rhythm itself. Healthy looks like a sawtooth: vertical steps up at each shot, slopes down between them, and one long overnight slide to the morning low. A flat trace means over-watering (shallow dryback), and one zone diverging from its siblings means a blocked dripper, a leak or a failing sensor in that row.

Plant-State AI.title: AI insight panel
Plant-State AI.body: Short interpretive cards built from the live state — hotspot attribution, dryback pattern, how pore EC is converging on target — each one expandable into a fuller explanation. The Ask AI co-pilot underneath hands the entire live room state (zones, snapshot, open advisories) to Claude, so you can ask why something is happening, not just what the number is. Treat its suggestions as a second opinion, not an actuator — it never writes to the system.

All zones · live state & setpoints.title: Zone cockpit overview
All zones · live state & setpoints.body: Each card is one irrigation zone (one row of plants with its own valve and sensors) showing live state next to the setpoints that drive it. Everything here is live: changing a select or number writes straight to Home Assistant and the engine acts on it — there is no save button and no undo. The full recipe editor lives in Tune and raw hardware switching in Operate.

Phase.title: Zone irrigation phase
Phase.body: Which of the four daily phases this zone is in. P0: after lights-on, wait for the substrate to dry back from its overnight peak. P1: progressive ramp shots up to the moisture target. P2: the main day phase, topping up whenever VWC drops below the re-water threshold. P3: pre-lights-off and overnight, emergency-only, letting the substrate dry back for the next morning. Zones walk the cycle independently, and lights-off forces any zone to P3 so nothing strands mid-cycle.

Status.title: Zone status word
Status.body: The zone's health verdict, combining moisture position and the engine's safety status. Critical means VWC is at or below the emergency floor; warning means within 6 percent of the floor or flagged as approaching saturation (looking wet — ease off the shots if it is a real trend). The note underneath is the engine's own safety message, like a blocked-dripper or sensor fault flag.

Zone enabled.title: Include zone in automation
Zone enabled.body: Master include/exclude for this zone: off means the engine skips it completely — no planned shots, no emergency rescue, nothing. Use it for an empty row or a row being torn down, not as a pause button; a planted row left disabled will simply dry out. Toggling it here writes live to the system.

VWC.title: Zone moisture and tank graphic
VWC.body: The zone's fused substrate moisture in percent, drawn as a tank: the fill is the current VWC on a 20-75 percent scale and the red line is the emergency floor. The number stays green while comfortably above the floor, turns amber within 6 percent of it, and red at or below it — at which point the engine should already be firing rescue shots. Watch the fill ride down toward the floor through P3 overnight; that slide is by design.

pore EC.title: Zone pore EC
pore EC.body: Salt concentration in this zone's substrate, in mS/cm, fused from the row's probes. Compare it across the three zones: one row stacking much higher than its siblings usually means it is getting less water (blocked dripper, low plant count weighting) and one much lower means it is channelling or running off. The target it should converge on is the EC target P2 setpoint below.

db / tgt.title: Dryback so far vs target
db / tgt.body: How many percentage points this zone has dried back from its last peak, against the target for its steering mode. Dryback is always measured as a drop FROM peak, not a level it dries to. On target means the steering recipe is being executed; chronically shallow means shots are landing too often or too big, and the salt-stacking effect that depends on the dryback will lag.

Peak VWC.title: Detected post-shot peak
Peak VWC.body: The high-water mark the engine detected after the last irrigation — the reference point every dryback number is measured from. Today's dryback is simply this peak minus the current VWC. A peak that never reaches the usual level on one zone suggests under-delivery there (clogged emitters or a low shot multiplier).

L today.title: Water delivered today
L today.body: Litres this zone has received since lights-on — the counter resets at the P3-to-P0 transition (the real grow-day boundary), not at midnight. Divide by the zone's plant count for litres per plant, and read it with the shot count. A sudden jump means a leak or runaway watering; near zero in the middle of the day means a blocked line, a disabled zone, or the engine refusing shots — check the activity feed for why.

Last shot.title: Time since last irrigation
Last shot.body: How long since the engine last watered this zone. During P2 in the light period expect a steady cadence as VWC bounces off the re-water threshold; a long gap during lights-on deserves a look at the activity feed. Long gaps overnight are normal — P3 is emergency-only by design.

next.title: Predicted next shot
next.body: The predictor's estimate of hours until this zone's next irrigation, based on the current dry-down rate and thresholds. It is a forecast, not a schedule — a hot afternoon shortens it, a cloudy dim period stretches it. 'Now' means the zone is at or past its trigger and a shot should be imminent.

All setpoints & 24h trend.title: Open the zone cockpit
All setpoints & 24h trend.body: Opens the full per-zone panel: every setpoint that drives this zone (with live context under each one), the maintenance switches, a wetness chart and an operator notes box. The same warning applies inside — every change writes live to the running system.

Phase pin.title: Manual phase override
Phase pin.body: Auto lets the engine walk the zone through P0-P3 by itself — the normal state. Picking a phase pins the zone there until you set it back to Auto, which is useful for testing or recovering a stuck zone (a known fix for a zone wedged near lights-on is pin P0, then back to Auto). A forgotten pin quietly breaks the daily cycle, so always return it to Auto.

Steering mode.title: Vegetative or generative bias
Steering mode.body: The per-zone steering direction, and the switch that decides which EC and dryback targets apply. Vegetative steers wet: shallower drybacks, more generous re-watering, lower EC targets — pushing leafy growth. Generative steers dry: deeper drybacks, smaller shots, higher EC — pushing flower development. Changing it immediately swaps the active target set for this zone.

Crop profile.title: Per-zone crop preset
Crop profile.body: A preset bundle of crop parameters for this zone. Follow Main means use the system-wide profile; picking a named profile (Athena-style cannabis presets and variants) lets one row run its own recipe — for example a different cultivar on row 2. Most of the time all zones should follow main unless you are deliberately running a comparison.

P2 re-water at.title: P2 top-up trigger
P2 re-water at.body: The VWC percentage that triggers a maintenance shot during P2: the substrate dries to this line, the engine waters, and the cycle repeats all day. Lower it to dry the zone harder (more generative), raise it to keep things wetter. Note the engine adjusts the effective trigger on its own: the EC-ratio logic and the autonomous EC-stacking loop both nudge it to chase the EC target, so the working value can differ slightly from what you set. The context line shows current VWC and the floor for reference.

EC target P2.title: Maintenance-phase EC target
EC target P2.body: The pore EC, in mS/cm, the engine drives this zone toward during P2 for its current steering mode — generative defaults around 6.0, vegetative around 3.2. With EC stacking enabled the engine closes the loop itself: it deepens dryback to concentrate salts when pore EC is below target and backs off when above. The context line shows current pore EC against the feed EC so you can see which direction the gap will move.

P2 shot size.title: Maintenance shot volume
P2 shot size.body: Size of each P2 top-up as a percentage of substrate volume per plant (default 5 percent — on a 6 L block that is 300 mL). The engine converts it to litres and then to valve-open seconds using the dripper flow rate. Small shots let EC stack between waterings; big shots push runoff and flush salt out. If VWC swings feel too large, go smaller and let the threshold trigger more often.

Dryback target.title: Target drop from peak
Dryback target.body: How many percentage points the zone should dry back from its post-irrigation peak — the core crop-steering lever. Deeper drybacks stress generatively and stack EC; shallower keeps the plant in comfortable vegetative growth. Remember the semantics: a 15 percent target on a 60 percent peak means drying to about 45, not to 15. The context line shows how far off peak the zone is right now; which target applies (veg or gen) follows the steering mode.

Emergency floor.title: Rescue-shot threshold
Emergency floor.body: The red line on the tank graphic: if VWC falls to this level at any time, including overnight in P3, the engine fires emergency rescue shots regardless of schedules or daily caps. It is the last defence against drying the roots out, and it also bounds how dry the autonomous EC-stacking loop is allowed to push. The context shows the predicted overnight low so you can see how close tonight will come; set it with margin below the normal overnight trough.

Manual override.title: Absolute valve lockout
Manual override.body: When on, nothing opens this zone's valve — not the schedule, not an emergency rescue, not a manual shot. It exists for maintenance: swapping a dripper line, recalibrating a probe, working on the row. Because it even blocks rescue watering, a forgotten override on a planted row is dangerous; turn it off the moment the work is done.

Dripper protection.title: Blocked-dripper back-off
Dripper protection.body: Lets the engine give up gracefully on a zone that takes repeated emergency shots without VWC rising — the signature of a blocked dripper or a row draining straight through. Instead of pumping water forever, the zone backs off and raises an alert (the limit is a set number of emergency shots in 30 minutes). Leave it on for normal running; the alternative is flooding a broken row all night.

24h wetness.title: Zone wetness sketch
24h wetness.body: A quick indicative wetness curve drawn around the zone's current reading — a feel for the shape of the day, not a recorded log. For the real measured 24-hour history of all three zones, use the Substrate last-24h chart on the Status tab, which pulls actual Home Assistant history.

Operator notes.title: Per-zone scratch notes
Operator notes.body: A free-text log for observations on this zone — what you saw, what you changed, what to check tomorrow. Notes save automatically as you type but live only in this browser's local storage: they are not written to Home Assistant and will not appear on another device.

Fused EC.title: Feed-vs-pore EC anchor
Fused EC.body: The sensor-fused pore EC across the room, shown here next to the irrigation context because it is the number the engine's EC logic runs on — the EC ratio that shifts re-water thresholds is this value divided by the active target. If one zone's own EC disagrees wildly with the fused number, suspect that zone's probe before suspecting the feed.

EC target.title: Active EC target
EC target.body: The pore-EC goal the dashboard judges readings against — 6.0 mS/cm, the generative P2 target this room is being steered to. The gap between fused EC and this number is what the EC-stacking loop is working to close: below target it deepens drybacks to concentrate salt, above target it eases off. Per-zone and per-phase targets are set in each zone's cockpit.

sensor.veg_main_pump_power.title: Live pump power draw
sensor.veg_main_pump_power.body: Watts the main irrigation pump is pulling right now. Zero means idle, which is the normal state between shots; a reading during a shot confirms water is actually moving. The number goes amber above 100 W — and over 100 W with all three row valves shut is a dead-head (pumping against a closed pipe), which raises an urgent advisory and warrants stopping the pump immediately.

sensor.crop_steering_app_next_irrigation.title: Engine's next planned shot
sensor.crop_steering_app_next_irrigation.body: The engine's predicted time for the next irrigation anywhere in the room, published by the decision loop itself. It moves as conditions change — faster dry-down pulls it earlier, a safety block or the dosing flag pushes it out. If it sits far in the future during the main light period, check the activity feed and the engine telemetry on Operate for what is holding water back.

Air temp.title: Room air temperature
Air temp.body: Air temperature from the SCD41 environment node, in degrees C. The on-target band is 24-26 C; the value turns amber outside roughly 22.5-27.5. Temperature drives VPD and transpiration, which sets how fast the substrate dries back - so a hot room steers the irrigation engine indirectly.

Humidity.title: Room relative humidity
Humidity.body: Relative humidity in percent, target 58-65%. Together with air temp it sets VPD: too dry pushes VPD up and stresses stomata, too humid invites condensation and mould. The dehumidifier bank below in the controls section is the main lever.

Air VPD.title: Air vapour pressure deficit
Air VPD.body: How hard the air is pulling moisture, in kPa, computed from room temp and RH. Target 1.0-1.4 kPa; below about 0.8 transpiration stalls and mould risk rises, above about 1.6 stomata close and growth slows. Adjust via temperature and dehumidification rather than chasing it directly.

Leaf VPD.title: Leaf vapour pressure deficit
Leaf VPD.body: VPD computed at the leaf surface using leaf temperature instead of air temperature - the actual transpiration driver the plant feels. Target 1.0-1.3 kPa. Under LEDs leaves usually run cooler than the air, so leaf VPD typically reads below air VPD; a leaf VPD well above air VPD points at airflow or heat stress.

CO₂.title: Room CO2 level
CO₂.body: CO2 concentration in ppm from the SCD41 node. Enriched target while lights are on is 1200-1450 ppm; in the dark the tile just shows the level since plants are not photosynthesising (sustained dark readings above ~1800 ppm are worth investigating as a leak or stuck injector). Injection is controlled by the CO2 switches and targets in the controls section below.

PPFD.title: Light intensity at canopy
PPFD.body: Photosynthetic photon flux density in umol/m2/s from the quantum sensor - how much usable light hits the canopy. With CO2 enrichment the working band is roughly 850-1200. Shows 'dark' outside the photoperiod, which is normal.

DLI today.title: Daily light integral
DLI today.body: Cumulative light delivered so far today, in mol/m2 - PPFD integrated over the photoperiod. A 1000 PPFD / 12 h flowering day lands around 43 mol; the counter resets at the hour configured on the sensor node (see Sensor calibration). If DLI is pacing low mid-day, a row of LEDs may be off or dimmed.

Back-left.title: Back-left hotspot temperature
Back-left.body: Dedicated temperature sensor in the back-left corner, which historically runs hot in F2. The tile turns amber when the corner reads 2.6 C or more above the room (watch level starts at 1.5 C). A persistent gradient that tracks lights-on points at internal airflow, not the AC - fix with a circulation fan before touching climate setpoints.

Climate trends · last 24h.title: 24-hour climate charts
Climate trends · last 24h.body: Real Home Assistant history for air temp, humidity, air VPD and CO2 over the last 24 hours, refetched about every 4 minutes. The shaded band on each chart is the on-target range (temp 24-26 C, RH 58-65%, VPD 1.0-1.4 kPa, CO2 1200-1450 ppm). Read these for drift and day/night patterns - a single bad tile is noise, a line living outside its band is a setpoint or equipment problem.

F2 room controls.title: Live room controls
F2 room controls.body: Every F2 climate control grouped by function: setpoints, AC, dehumidifiers, CO2, lights, access, plus collapsed housekeeping groups for thresholds, sensor mapping, calibration and device restarts. Everything here writes to Home Assistant immediately with no undo, so treat each toggle like a physical switch on the wall. Number tiles show a live 'now' chip with the on-target band so you can see current-vs-target while you adjust.

input_number.f2_setpoint_temp_day.title: Day temperature setpoint
input_number.f2_setpoint_temp_day.body: The room's daytime air-temperature target in degrees C, read by the F2 climate automations and shown against the live reading on this tile. The healthy band is 24-26 C. Move it in small steps - temperature shifts VPD and dryback speed, so a 2 C change also changes how often the irrigation engine wants to water.

input_number.f2_setpoint_rh.title: Humidity setpoint
input_number.f2_setpoint_rh.body: Target relative humidity in percent for the F2 room, with the live reading and 58-65% band shown on the tile. The dehumidifier bank works toward this. Lower it late in flower for mould protection, accepting higher VPD.

input_number.f2_setpoint_vpd.title: Air VPD setpoint
input_number.f2_setpoint_vpd.body: Target air VPD in kPa. The tile chips show the live air VPD against the 1.0-1.4 kPa band. VPD is the derived master variable - in practice you reach this target by trimming the temp and RH setpoints rather than this number alone.

input_number.f2_target_vpd.title: Leaf VPD target
input_number.f2_target_vpd.body: Target VPD at the leaf, in kPa, checked against the live leaf-VPD sensor (band 1.0-1.3). This is the more physiologically honest target than air VPD because it uses actual leaf temperature. Keep it slightly below the air VPD target since LED canopies run cool.

input_number.f2_setpoint_leaf_temp.title: Leaf temperature target
input_number.f2_setpoint_leaf_temp.body: Reference canopy leaf temperature in degrees C used by the climate logic and VPD math. LED canopies typically sit 1-2.5 C below air temp; a leaf running at or above air temp means poor transpiration or airflow. Used as a target, not a control output - the AC and airflow get you there.

input_number.f2_setpoint_co2.title: CO2 setpoint
input_number.f2_setpoint_co2.body: Reference CO2 target in ppm, shown against the live SCD41 reading and the 1200-1450 enriched band. The actual injection controller uses the day/night targets and tolerance in the CO2 group; keep this consistent with those so dashboards and automations agree.

input_number.f2_setpoint_ppfd.title: PPFD setpoint
input_number.f2_setpoint_ppfd.body: Target canopy light intensity in umol/m2/s, compared against the live quantum sensor (band 850-1200). It is the reference the dimming and advisory logic checks against - to actually change intensity, adjust the Row 1/2/3 LED brightness sliders in the Lighting group.

input_number.f2_setpoint_air_velocity.title: Air velocity target
input_number.f2_setpoint_air_velocity.body: Reference airflow speed across the canopy in m/s. Adequate air movement (roughly 0.5-1 m/s at canopy) keeps leaf temperature down and the boundary layer thin so leaf VPD stays honest. There is no live anemometer chip on this tile - it is a documented target for fan setup, and the back-left hotspot is the symptom to watch when airflow is short.

input_number.f2_setpoint_photoperiod.title: Photoperiod hours
input_number.f2_setpoint_photoperiod.body: Reference day length in hours for F2 (12 in flower). Note the irrigation engine takes its own photoperiod from number.crop_steering_lights_on_hour / lights_off_hour - if you change the room's light schedule, update those too or the P0-P3 phase clock will be misaligned with the actual lights.

input_number.f2_setpoint_pwec.title: Pore-water EC reference
input_number.f2_setpoint_pwec.body: Target substrate pore-water EC in mS/cm, shown against the live cross-zone average EC. This is the reference for climate-signal advisories; the irrigation engine's real EC targets are the per-phase number.crop_steering_ec_target_* entities edited in Tune. Generative steering on F2 runs pore EC around 6.

input_number.f2_setpoint_vwc.title: Substrate VWC reference
input_number.f2_setpoint_vwc.body: Reference substrate moisture in percent VWC, compared against the live fused VWC on the tile chip. Display/advisory reference only - the numbers that actually drive watering are the P1 target and P2 threshold per zone (Tune and Zones tabs). Useful as the 'where the room should be sitting mid-day' marker.

climate.f2_ac_pid.title: AC PID controller
climate.f2_ac_pid.body: The coordinating PID thermostat layered over the two Fujitsu mini-splits - it modulates them toward one room temperature target instead of letting two heads fight. Prefer setting mode and temperature here; only drive the individual wall/door units when testing hardware. Mode buttons write hvac_mode immediately and the +/- steps the target.

climate.f2_ac_wall_f2_ac_wall.title: Wall mini-split (raw)
climate.f2_ac_wall_f2_ac_wall.body: Direct control of the wall-mounted Fujitsu mini-split via its ESPHome IR controller - mode (cool/heat/dry/fan), target temperature and the current reading. This bypasses the PID coordinator, so a manual setting here can be overridden or can fight the other head. Use for diagnosis; if it stops responding, its restart switch is in Device restarts.

climate.f2_ac_door_f2_ac_door.title: Door mini-split (raw)
climate.f2_ac_door_f2_ac_door.body: Direct control of the door-side Fujitsu mini-split, same ESPHome IR path as the wall unit. Useful for forcing extra cooling at the hot end of the room or testing the head in isolation. Settings made here sit outside the PID coordinator and may be walked back by it.

humidifier.f2_dehumidifier.title: Dehumidifier target
humidifier.f2_dehumidifier.body: The HA humidifier entity for F2 dehumidification - set the target RH and on/off here. Works with the RH setpoint: if room RH lives above target while this is on, the bank is out of capacity for the current transpiration load (more plants transpiring than litres/day removed) and you need more units running or a lower night temperature.

switch.f2_dehumidifiers_group.title: Dehumidifier bank master
switch.f2_dehumidifiers_group.body: Master power for the F2 dehumidifier bank; the four numbered relay switches beside it (f2_dehumidifiers_relay_1 through _4) power each unit individually. Run the number of units the load needs - all four for peak lights-on transpiration, fewer at night. Killing the whole group in flower risks an overnight RH spike and condensation on the canopy.

switch.f2_dehumidifiers_relay_1.title: Dehumidifier unit relay
switch.f2_dehumidifiers_relay_1.body: Power relay for one physical dehumidifier in the F2 bank (relays 1-4 each feed one unit). Stage units on or off to match the moisture load; a unit that draws no effect when switched on is iced up, full, or failed. The group switch above overrides all four.

switch.f2_co2.title: F2 CO2 injection enable
switch.f2_co2.body: Master enable for CO2 injection into F2 - the gas solenoid only fires while this is on and the controller is below target. Turn it off when the room is open for work (CO2 displaces oxygen at floor level) or during dark hours if the controller does not gate on lights itself. The paired f2_co2_led switch is the controller's indicator channel.

input_number.f2_co2_day_target_input.title: CO2 day target
input_number.f2_co2_day_target_input.body: The ppm the CO2 controller holds while lights are on - typically 1200-1400 for enriched flowering at high PPFD. Raising it only pays if light, temperature and VWC can keep up (CO2 is rarely the limiting factor below 1000 PPFD). The injection stops when the room reads target plus tolerance.

input_number.f2_co2_night_target_input.title: CO2 night target
input_number.f2_co2_night_target_input.body: The ppm allowed during the dark period - plants do not use CO2 in the dark, so this is normally near-ambient (400-500). A high night target wastes gas and pushes the dark reading toward the 1800 ppm warning level. If night readings climb on their own, suspect a leaking solenoid rather than this setting.

input_number.f2_co2_tolerance_input.title: CO2 control deadband
input_number.f2_co2_tolerance_input.body: How far below target the room may drift before injection re-fires, in ppm - the controller's hysteresis. Too tight (under ~50) makes the solenoid chatter and wears it out; too loose makes the level saw-tooth visibly on the CO2 chart. The single f2_co2_target_input beside it is the legacy combined target some automations still read - keep it matching the day target.

input_boolean.f2_co2_tank_empty.title: CO2 tank status flags
input_boolean.f2_co2_tank_empty.body: Operator flag set when the CO2 tank runs out, with the companion f2_co2_tank_replaced flag for when a fresh tank goes on. These drive the tank-tracking and reminder automations - flip empty on when injection stops achieving target, then mark replaced after the swap so usage tracking restarts. They do not switch any hardware themselves.

light.led_f2_r1_f2_r1_led.title: Row 1 LED
light.led_f2_r1_f2_r1_led.body: The LED fixture bank over Row 1 (irrigation Zone 1), with on/off and a brightness slider that writes brightness_pct live. Dimming a single row is how you trim PPFD for that row only - remember light load also sets that zone's transpiration, so a dimmed row dries back slower and the engine waters it less.

light.led_f2_r2_f2_r2_led.title: Row 2 LED
light.led_f2_r2_f2_r2_led.body: The LED fixture bank over Row 2 (irrigation Zone 2), on/off plus brightness. This fixture also exposes the LED power sensor the dashboard shows in raw readings. Keep rows matched in intensity unless you are deliberately steering one row differently.

light.office_light_main_spectrum.title: Row 3 LED (F2)
light.office_light_main_spectrum.body: The main-spectrum LED over Row 3 (irrigation Zone 3) - the entity id still says 'office' from where the fixture was first commissioned, but it is physically in F2, hence the relabel. On/off and brightness like the other rows. If Row 3's zone dries back differently from Rows 1-2, check this fixture's level first.

light.under_canopy_1_veg_undercanopy_lig.title: Under-canopy light
light.under_canopy_1_veg_undercanopy_lig.body: Supplemental under-canopy LED in F2 (entity named for veg, where it lived before being moved). Lights the lower third of the plant to wake up larf-prone bud sites. Modest PPFD contribution - it will not move the room's quantum sensor much since that reads at canopy top.

switch.f2_lights_contactor_ssr_f1_lights.title: Lights power contactor
switch.f2_lights_contactor_ssr_f1_lights.body: The solid-state contactor feeding the room's main light circuit - the hard power switch upstream of the dimmable fixtures. Off kills every row regardless of their dimmer state. The irrigation engine does not watch this switch (it runs on the configured lights-on/off hours), so a contactor left off during the day quietly breaks the dryback the engine expects.

Room & access.title: Door and camera detection
Room & access.body: Room access and detection switches: f2_door_relay drives the door release, and f2_detect_2 / f2_motion_2 toggle the F2 camera's object-detection and motion processing in the NVR. Turn detection off during long work sessions to stop alert spam, and back on when you leave. The door relay is live hardware - one tap actuates it.

Climate-Sense thresholds.title: Advisory trigger thresholds
Climate-Sense thresholds.body: The csig_f2_* numbers set where the climate-signal advisories fire: high VPD, high EC, high PPFD, root-temp high/low, low VWC, minimum cooling, warm-leaf delta, CO2 enrichment gates and the CWSI baseline slope/intercept. They tune alerts and insight cards only - the irrigation engine does not read them. Widen a threshold if a known-benign condition keeps nagging; tighten it if a real problem arrived without warning.

Sensor mapping.title: Sensor source mapping
Sensor mapping.body: Each input_text holds the entity id of the physical sensor feeding one climate signal (air temp, humidity, CO2, PPFD, leaf temp, root temp, EC, VWC). Edit these to repoint a signal at a different sensor - after a hardware swap, for example - without touching code. A typo here makes that signal read blank or stale, so paste exact entity ids.

Sensor calibration.title: Sensor calibration tools
Sensor calibration.body: Calibration offsets and actions for the F2 sensor nodes: temperature/humidity offsets and references for the entrance AirQ unit, the SCD41 forced-recalibration (FRC) reference, PPFD/lux two-point calibration, and the DLI reset time. The buttons fire one-shot calibrations immediately - only press them with a trusted reference instrument in place, because a bad FRC skews every CO2 reading and decision downstream.

Camera & recording.title: Camera recording toggles
Camera & recording.body: NVR switches for the F2 camera: continuous recordings, snapshots, and review alerts/detections. Disable temporarily during maintenance to keep the review feed clean; leave recordings on otherwise - the footage is the audit trail when sensors and plants disagree.

Device restarts.title: Device restart switches
Device restarts.body: Reboot switches and buttons for the room's ESPHome nodes: both AC IR controllers, the dehumidifier board, the SCD41 sensor node, both LED controllers, and the AirQ display. A restart is the first fix for a device showing unavailable or frozen values; expect that device's readings or control to drop out for roughly a minute while it reconnects.

Room map — hotspot watch.title: Room hotspot map
Room map — hotspot watch.body: A schematic of the room's trouble spots: the separately-sensed back-left corner versus room temperature, plus each zone's VWC chip coloured by status. The back-left cell flips to a flame and amber when the corner runs 1.5 C (watch) or 2.6 C (warn) above the room. It is a layout-shaped summary of the same data as the tiles above, useful for spotting which physical corner or row is the outlier.

All raw readings.title: Raw sensor list
All raw readings.body: The unprocessed sensor list behind the tiles: air metrics, the Row 1 reference block's wetness / pore-EC / temperature, back-left readings, fused VWC, average EC, sensor health, next predicted watering, the engine's last decision and the LED power draw. Values turn amber when outside their band. Use it to cross-check a suspicious tile against the underlying sensors.

Facility ops.title: Operate tab warning
Facility ops.body: Everything on this tab writes to Home Assistant immediately - there is no undo or staging. Strategy, arm switches and adaptive numbers change live irrigation behaviour the moment you touch them. Per-zone setpoints live in the Zones tab and the full recipe editor in Tune; this tab is the facility-level console and raw hardware access.

sensor.crop_steering_app_current_phase.title: Current phase per zone
sensor.crop_steering_app_current_phase.body: The engine's live phase for each zone, like Z1:P2, Z2:P2, Z3:P3. The daily cycle is P0 morning dryback, P1 ramp-up shots, P2 maintenance top-ups, P3 emergency-only before and through lights-off. Zones move independently; one zone sitting in a different phase from its siblings for hours is worth a look at its sensors.

Dryback.title: Active dryback target
Dryback.body: The overnight dryback target that applies to the current steering mode - generative_dryback_target or vegetative_dryback_target (defaults 40% gen / 50% veg, tuned lower on F2). Dryback is always a percentage-point drop FROM peak VWC, never a dry-back-to level. Deeper dryback concentrates salts and steers generative; shallow keeps the block wet and steers vegetative.

number.crop_steering_field_capacity.title: Field capacity ceiling
number.crop_steering_field_capacity.body: The VWC at which the substrate is full and extra water becomes runoff (default 60%, F2 tuned per block). The engine blocks any shot at or above it - the over-watering guard - and P1 ramps are clamped under it. If real runoff appears well before this number, the setting is too high for your blocks and the engine will overestimate how much water fits.

select.crop_steering_steering_mode.title: Steering mode
select.crop_steering_steering_mode.body: The system-wide vegetative/generative bias. It picks which EC targets and which dryback target the engine applies everywhere a zone doesn't override it: vegetative means wetter, shallower drybacks and leaner EC for structure growth; generative means deeper drybacks and higher EC to push flowering. This is the single biggest lever on the tab - change it deliberately, usually at a stage transition.

select.crop_steering_growth_stage.title: Growth stage
select.crop_steering_growth_stage.body: Where the crop is in its life - Vegetative, Generative or Transition. It shifts the EC target tables and dryback aggressiveness alongside steering mode. Keep it matched to the calendar; a room flagged Vegetative in week 5 of flower will be fed and watered too soft.

switch.crop_steering_system_enabled.title: System master switch
switch.crop_steering_system_enabled.body: Master on/off for the whole crop-steering system. Off means no irrigation at all - no scheduled shots, no emergency rescue - so it is the maintenance lockout, not a pause button. The UI asks for confirmation because flipping this directly changes whether plants get water. The emergency stop turns this off as its first action.

switch.crop_steering_auto_irrigation_ena.title: Auto irrigation switch
switch.crop_steering_auto_irrigation_ena.body: Enables the autonomous decision loop. On, the engine decides and fires shots by itself; off, the system still tracks sensors and phases but only waters when you trigger shots manually. Use off for supervised days (sensor swaps, tuning sessions) where you want eyes on every shot.

input_boolean.f2_flush_mode.title: Flush mode
input_boolean.f2_flush_mode.body: Manual pump mode for flushing - while on, the engine treats the pump as yours and stops steering veg_main_pump entirely, so no autonomous irrigation runs. A phone reminder fires every 30 minutes until you switch it back off. Use for line flushes and low-EC reset waterings; forgetting it on is effectively pausing the system.

input_boolean.f2_fill_mode.title: Fill mode
input_boolean.f2_fill_mode.body: Manual pump mode for tank filling or transfers - same engine behaviour as flush mode: autonomous irrigation is suspended while it is on and a 30-minute reminder nags until it is off. Flip it on before manual pump work so the engine cannot start a shot mid-task, and off as soon as you are done.

Relays & solenoids.title: Raw hardware switching
Relays & solenoids.body: Every irrigation relay, solenoid and pump as a direct on/off - this is below the engine, with no interlocks except a confirm on the dangerous rows. The water path is pump, then mainline/manifold, then a zone valve, then section drip solenoids to the drippers; the engine always sequences pump prime (2 s), mainline (1 s), zone valve, irrigate, then shutdown in reverse. Each row shows state age, live watts where metered, the next predicted shot and today's delivery. The Stop all button opens the emergency-stop panel.

switch.espoe_main_pump_empty.title: Veg pump (empty/transfer)
switch.espoe_main_pump_empty.body: Second pump channel on the irrigation ESP board, labelled Veg pump and named for tank-empty/transfer duty. It is not part of the engine's shot sequence - the engine only drives veg_main_pump. Same physics apply: confirm an open flow path before switching it on, or it dead-heads.

switch.pump_power_switch.title: Pump power feed
switch.pump_power_switch.body: The metered power feed upstream of the pump - the hard electrical cut, with its own watts sensor. It must stay on for the engine to run the pump at all; switching it off is the lockout you use when working on the pump, because it overrides everything downstream including a stuck-on pump relay.

switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_1.title: Manifold solenoid
switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_1.body: Opens the distribution manifold between the pump and the line solenoids. With it shut, nothing downstream sees water no matter which valves are open; with it open and a pump running but no line/zone open, pressure dead-ends here. Manual use is mostly leak-testing sections of the plumbing.

switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_2.title: Veg mains solenoid
switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_2.body: The veg mains feed solenoid - one of the two line valves between the manifold and the room's zone valves. Open it together with the manifold and a zone valve when manually watering through this branch. Leaving it open does not flow water by itself, but it removes one barrier between a stuck pump and the benches.

switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_3.title: Veg mainline solenoid
switch.espoe_irrigation_relay_2_3.body: THE mainline solenoid in the engine's shot sequence - it opens one second after pump prime and before the zone valve on every automatic shot, and the emergency stop closes it. If shots are being commanded but no water arrives anywhere, watch whether this relay actually clicks on. Manually opening it with the pump on sends pressure to whatever zone valves are open.

switch.f2_row1.title: Zone 1 valve (Row 1)
switch.f2_row1.body: The zone valve for Row 1 - the engine opens exactly one of the three row valves per shot, and verifies it closed again afterwards (a failed close triggers an emergency pump stop). Manually opening it with the pump and mainline on waters Row 1 outside the engine's accounting, so daily litres and shot counts for the zone will under-read. Leaving it open invites flooding on the next pump start.

switch.f2_row2.title: Zone 2 valve (Row 2)
switch.f2_row2.body: The zone valve for Row 2, engine-driven one-at-a-time per shot with close verification. The row shows the zone's next predicted shot and today's litres so you can see what the engine has planned before overriding. Manual watering through it bypasses the zone's volume tracking - log it mentally or expect the daily numbers to lie low.

switch.f2_row3.title: Zone 3 valve (Row 3)
switch.f2_row3.body: The zone valve for Row 3, same behaviour as Rows 1-2: engine-sequenced, read-back verified, shut by the emergency stop. If Row 3 alone looks dry while its valve cycles normally, look at drippers or the Row 3 LED level rather than this relay.

switch.veg_solenoid_relays_lt.title: Drip solenoid left-top
switch.veg_solenoid_relays_lt.body: Section solenoid for the left-top bench drip lines, downstream of the zone valves. It selects which drip runs are live: shut, that section's drippers get nothing even during a valid shot; opened in addition to others, the shot's volume spreads thinner than the engine calculated. Normally leave these in their standing position and only touch them for section isolation or flushing.

switch.veg_solenoid_relays_lb.title: Drip solenoid left-bottom
switch.veg_solenoid_relays_lb.body: Section solenoid for the left-bottom bench drip lines. Use it to isolate that section for repairs or to flush one bench at a time. A section accidentally left shut shows up as one bench of dry blocks while the zone's sensors elsewhere read normal.

switch.veg_solenoid_right_top.title: Drip solenoid right-top
switch.veg_solenoid_right_top.body: Section solenoid for the right-top bench drip lines. Same rules as the other quarters: it gates flow to its drippers, so check its state when one bench section is mysteriously dry or soaked. Shutting it while a shot runs dead-ends that branch's share of the flow.

switch.veg_solenoid_relays_r_b.title: Drip solenoid right-bottom
switch.veg_solenoid_relays_r_b.body: Section solenoid for the right-bottom bench drip lines - the fourth quarter of the drip distribution. Useful for halving or quartering the irrigated area during partial-room cycles. Confirm all intended sections are open before manual pump runs.

switch.veg_solenoid_relays_waste.title: Waste solenoid (legacy)
switch.veg_solenoid_relays_waste.body: One of the waste-line solenoid channels that diverts flow to drain instead of the benches - this is the older relay channel; the one labelled Waste (current) is the actively-plumbed one. Waste routing is for line flushes and dumping stale mix. Open during a feed shot means nutrient straight down the drain.

switch.veg_solenoid_waste.title: Waste solenoid (current)
switch.veg_solenoid_waste.body: The currently-plumbed waste solenoid - open it to send pump output to the drain for line flushing or dumping a tank. Always pair it with a pump source and shut it again afterwards: left open it silently drains every subsequent shot, and the zones' VWC will not rise despite watering being logged.

switch.waste_valve.title: Waste valve
switch.waste_valve.body: The drain-side waste valve completing the waste path. Open together with the waste solenoid when flushing; shut for normal operation. If runoff or flush water is backing up, this is the valve to check before suspecting a blocked drain line.

switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_core_pump.title: Core dosing pump
switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_core_pump.body: Power to the peristaltic pump metering the Core stock concentrate (the Athena base mineral line) into the mix. The dosing automation pulses it; switched on manually it runs continuously and can overshoot the feed EC within minutes. Only run it by hand for priming the tube or calibrating ml/min into a measuring cup - and watch the tank EC after.

switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_bloom_pum.title: Bloom dosing pump
switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_bloom_pum.body: Power to the Bloom concentrate peristaltic pump - the flowering-side base nutrient. Same rules as Core: automation pulses it, manual on means continuous dosing and a real overdose risk. If feed EC trends high day after day, check this pump's calibration before blaming the recipe.

switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_balance_p.title: Balance dosing pump
switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_balance_p.body: Power to the peristaltic pump dosing Balance, the pH/alkalinity buffer. Small volumes move pH a long way, so a stuck-on Balance pump wrecks the irrigation pH gate (5.8-6.2) quickly and the engine will then refuse to water on source-water grounds. Manual use is priming and calibration only.

switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_cleanse_p.title: Cleanse dosing pump
switch.espoe_peristaltic_pumps_cleanse_p.body: Power to the peristaltic pump dosing Cleanse, the hypochlorous line/water sanitiser. It keeps drip lines and the root zone clean at maintenance rates; continuous manual running over-chlorinates the tank and can stress roots. Prime or calibrate by hand, dose by automation.

switch.veg_co2_relay_veg_co2_relay.title: Veg CO2 relay
switch.veg_co2_relay_veg_co2_relay.body: The CO2 injection relay for the veg room's supply solenoid (F2's own injection is the f2_co2 switch on the Climate tab). On means the controller may inject; forcing it on manually floods CO2 regardless of the room reading. Treat it as a safety device - off before anyone works in a small closed room.

input_boolean.f2_adaptive_steering_enabl.title: Adaptive steering master
input_boolean.f2_adaptive_steering_enabl.body: Enables the adaptive layer that learns each zone's real saturation point (Vmax) from P1 behaviour, derives the P2 threshold as Vmax times (1 minus dryback%), and ramps the P1 target up day-over-day as plants grow. With it off the engine no-ops the whole adaptive module and just uses your static setpoints. Watch sensor.crop_steering_zone_N_vmax_detected to see what it has learned before trusting it.

input_number.f2_vmax_confidence_min.title: Vmax confidence minimum
input_number.f2_vmax_confidence_min.body: How sure the Vmax detector must be (0.3-1.0, default 0.6) before it locks the day's detected saturation ceiling and applies the derived P2 threshold. Confidence is the fraction of detection signals that agree - plateau, runoff onset, curve fit. Raise it if the detector locks silly values on noisy sensor days; lower it if it never locks at all.

input_number.f2_p1_climb_rate_per_day.title: P1 climb per day
input_number.f2_p1_climb_rate_per_day.body: How much the per-zone P1 target VWC is allowed to rise each day, in percentage points (0-5, default 1.0). This is the growth ramp - bigger plants drink more, so the morning re-wet target creeps up automatically. The climb is capped at the lower of detected Vmax and field capacity, minus the margin below.

input_number.f2_p1_margin.title: P1 margin below Vmax
input_number.f2_p1_margin.body: The safety gap in VWC points (0-6, default 2.0) kept between the ramped P1 target and the zone's ceiling (the lower of detected Vmax and field capacity). It stops the ramp from targeting outright saturation, where every extra shot just becomes runoff. Widen it if you see runoff at the end of P1 ramps; tighten it to push the wet end harder.

Zones.title: All zones at a glance
Zones.body: One-line cross-zone summary: how many zones are enabled and each zone's live VWC and phase, coloured by the worst zone status. It is a jump card - tap it to manage per-zone setpoints, enable switches, overrides and phase pins in the Zones tab. Three zones in the same phase with similar VWC is the healthy picture; an outlier is your first stop.

Guardrails & recipe.title: Guardrails and recipe link
Guardrails & recipe.body: The safety guardrails (EC and pH gates, substrate EC cutoff, blocked-dripper cap) and the full irrigation recipe are edited in the Tune tab - this card jumps there. Guardrails are the limits the engine refuses to cross no matter what the recipe asks for.

number.crop_steering_maximum_ec.title: Max substrate EC cutoff
number.crop_steering_maximum_ec.body: Hard substrate-EC ceiling in mS/cm (default 9.0) - the salt-burn guard. No shot fires into a zone reading above it, which protects roots when EC stacking or under-watering has pushed pore EC too high. If zones keep hitting this cutoff, the fix is a deliberate flush or leaner feed, not raising the cutoff.

number.crop_steering_irrigation_ec_min.title: Source EC gate (low)
number.crop_steering_irrigation_ec_min.body: The low bound of the source-water EC gate, in mS/cm (default 2.3, 0 disables). If the feed water reads below it the engine blocks all irrigation rather than water with the wrong mix - typically a dosing failure or a tank of plain water. Paired with irrigation_ec_max (default 3.5) for the high side. A room mysteriously not watering is often this gate doing its job.

number.crop_steering_irrigation_ec_max.title: Source EC gate (high)
number.crop_steering_irrigation_ec_max.body: The high bound of the source-water EC gate (default 3.5 mS/cm). Feed reading above it blocks irrigation - protection against a dosing pump stuck on or a double-strength batch. Check the activity feed for blocked events naming source EC when zones skip expected shots.

number.crop_steering_irrigation_ph_min.title: Source pH gate (low)
number.crop_steering_irrigation_ph_min.body: Low bound of the source-water pH gate (default 5.8). The engine will not irrigate while feed pH is below it, since out-of-range pH locks out nutrients in coco. With irrigation_ph_max (default 6.2) it defines the acceptable feed window; persistent blocks here mean the pH dosing or probe needs attention, not the irrigation.

number.crop_steering_irrigation_ph_max.title: Source pH gate (high)
number.crop_steering_irrigation_ph_max.body: High bound of the source-water pH gate (default 6.2). Feed above it blocks watering until the mix is corrected. If the probe has drifted it can false-block the whole room - cross-check with a handheld meter before trusting a sudden string of pH blocks.

number.crop_steering_lights_on_hour.title: Lights-on / off window
number.crop_steering_lights_on_hour.body: The photoperiod boundaries the engine runs its clock on: lights_on_hour (default 10) and lights_off_hour (default 22). Lights-on is the grow-day boundary - P3 zones transition to P0 and the daily water/shot counters reset there, and lights-off forces any P1/P2 zone into P3 for the night. These must match the actual light schedule or every phase and counter is offset.

number.crop_steering_blocked_dripper_max.title: Blocked-dripper shot cap
number.crop_steering_blocked_dripper_max.body: How many emergency shots a zone may take within 30 minutes (default 4) before the engine flags it as a blocked or free-draining dripper and backs off instead of pouring more water in. It is the runaway-rescue brake: repeated shots with no VWC response means the water is not reaching the sensor. When a zone trips this, walk the row and check drippers and the block before re-enabling.

sensor.crop_steering_app_status.title: Engine app status
sensor.crop_steering_app_status.body: The AppDaemon engine's own status report - active when the loop is running, safe_idle when it has parked itself. Unknown or stale here means the engine process is down and nothing autonomous is happening, regardless of what the switches say. First check when the room has gone quiet: AppDaemon logs.

sensor.crop_steering_system_state.title: System state
sensor.crop_steering_system_state.body: The integration-side system state alongside app status - the two should agree that the system is active and safe. A fault state here while the app reports active points at a safety condition (valve read-back, watchdog) rather than a crashed process. The activity feed below usually names the trigger.

sensor.crop_steering_current_decision.title: Current engine decision
sensor.crop_steering_current_decision.body: What the decision loop concluded on its latest cycle - irrigate, wait, or a named block reason. It is the engine thinking out loud: 'wait' with a healthy dryback in progress is normal, while repeated blocks name exactly which gate (source water, daily cap, max EC) is holding shots back.

sensor.crop_steering_ai_heartbeat.title: AI heartbeat
sensor.crop_steering_ai_heartbeat.body: Status of the engine's self-correction loop, with minutes since it last reported. It force-advances any phase stuck more than 4 hours, flags stale sensors, and flags zones that take water without VWC rising (drain-through or channelling). Healthy is the only good value - anything else turns the row amber and deserves a look at the activity feed; a heartbeat that stops aging up means the engine itself is down.

sensor.crop_steering_p2_vwc_threshold_ad.title: P2 threshold (adjusted)
sensor.crop_steering_p2_vwc_threshold_ad.body: The P2 maintenance re-watering threshold actually in force after the EC-ratio adjustment, in % VWC. When pore EC runs above target the engine raises the threshold (water more, flush salts); below target it lowers it (let it stack). Comparing this to the raw p2_vwc_threshold shows how hard the EC logic is leaning on the watering frequency.

sensor.crop_steering_prediction_estimate.title: Estimated daily water
sensor.crop_steering_prediction_estimate.body: The ML predictor's estimate of total litres the room will want this grow-day. Sanity-check it against the per-zone max_daily_volume caps and what is in the tank - a forecast above the caps means the engine will hit budget limits late in the day. Wildly wrong estimates usually follow big setpoint or plant-count changes and settle within a few days.

Recent activity.title: Engine activity feed
Recent activity.body: The rolling feed from sensor.crop_steering_activity_log - every watered, blocked and phase-change event in plain language, newest first. This is the ground truth for 'did it water and why/why not'. Read it before changing any setting: most mysteries (skipped shots, surprise phase moves) are already explained here.

Pump & irrigation.title: Emergency pump panel
Pump & irrigation.body: The manual/emergency pump panel, always one tap away. It shows live main-pump watts with a dead-head alarm - pump pressurised while every valve is shut - plus direct toggles for the pump, mainline and three zone valves with state-age and delivery info, and the software gates. Everything writes live with no undo; it exists so you can stop water in seconds without hunting through tabs.

EMERGENCY STOP — ALL OFF.title: Emergency stop button
EMERGENCY STOP — ALL OFF.body: One press (plus confirm) disables the system master and auto irrigation, then shuts the main pump, the mainline solenoid and all three zone valves in sequence - all watering halts immediately. After firing it, confirm the pump power readout drops to 0 W; if watts persist, cut the pump power feed switch. Re-arming afterwards is manual: turn the system and auto switches back on deliberately.

System gates.title: Software irrigation gates
System gates.body: The software interlocks shown beside the hardware: System enabled (master), Auto irrigation (the autonomous loop), and Dosing active (input_boolean.nutrient_dosing_active, set while the nutrient doser is running). They gate behaviour rather than switch water directly - hardware can still be driven manually with gates off, which is exactly what makes them the safe way to pause automation during maintenance.

Climate.title: Climate tab — environment
Climate.body: Room camera, 24-hour temp / RH / VPD / CO2 charts with the on-target band shaded, then every climate control: AC units, dehumidifiers, CO2, lighting, calibration. Also shows the back-left hotspot tracking. Climate here is monitoring and manual control — the irrigation engine never touches climate hardware.

Operate.title: Operate tab — live switches
Operate.body: The ops surface: an all-zones-at-a-glance grid plus the live crop-steering switches — system enable, auto-irrigation arm, steering mode, per-zone enables and overrides, safety guardrails and the activity feed. Everything here writes straight to Home Assistant, so changes take effect on the running engine immediately. Use it for day-to-day driving; use Tune for setpoint surgery.

Tune.title: Tune tab — setpoint editor
Tune.body: The interactive crop-steering editor: it seeds every P0-P3 setpoint from live Home Assistant, lets you adjust and simulate the resulting irrigation day, then shows a diff and pushes only the changed values back. Safer than editing numbers blind in HA because you preview the consequence first. Changes apply to the live engine once you confirm.

Plan.title: Plan tab — grow timeline
Plan.body: The whole-crop recipe planner: phases from Clone to Flush across a default 84-day grow, with every environment, lighting, root-zone and irrigation setpoint laid out per phase. It is a plan, not the live system — nothing changes in the room until you push a week to Home Assistant with HA Sync. Recipes are saved in this browser, so export JSON for backup.

3D Floor.title: 3D Floor — facility twin
3D Floor.body: An interactive Three.js model of the facility with live room states, lights, climate and per-row sensors. The model is about 34 MB so it loads only when you tap Load 3D view. Click a room card to control it; tapping a sensor tile jumps you to the matching dashboard tab.

Search · ask AI.title: Command palette (Cmd-K)
Search · ask AI.body: Quick-jump search: press Ctrl/Cmd-K or the slash key to open it, then type to find zones (with live VWC), views or actions like Ask AI and Read aloud. Hit Enter to run the highlighted result. Fastest way to move around without touching the sidebar.

Advisories.title: Advisories bell
Advisories.body: The bell shows how many advisories are active and jumps you to the advisories list on Status. Advisories are prescriptive: each one states the problem, the evidence, a confidence level and the next step — acknowledge one to clear it. Red badge means at least one needs action; an empty bell means the room is running clean.

Pump & irrigation — manual stop.title: Pump e-stop button
Pump & irrigation — manual stop.body: Opens the manual pump panel: live main-pump power, individual hardware toggles (pump, main line, three zone valves) and the big EMERGENCY STOP — ALL OFF button, which disables the system and auto-irrigation switches and then shuts the pump, main line and all valves. The header button pulses red with a power badge if the pump is dead-heading (pressurised against closed valves) — stop it immediately. Everything in this panel writes live to Home Assistant with no undo.

Grower.title: User chip
Grower.body: Shows who is logged into Home Assistant right now — the dashboard reads your HA user name and initials live, nothing is hardcoded. Purely informational; it does not change permissions.

Ask AI.title: AI co-pilot panel
Ask AI.body: Opens the Plant-State AI chat — a Claude model that is handed the live room context (headline, sensor snapshot, every zone's VWC/EC/phase, active advisories) and answers only from that data. Ask about a zone, dryback, EC or the hotspot; suggestion chips give starting questions. If the AI service is unreachable (it lives on the facility LAN) you get a local rule-based readout instead, clearly flagged.

Read aloud.title: Read aloud button
Read aloud.body: Uses your device's built-in speech synthesis to read the room headline (or an advisory, via its own Read button) out loud. Handy with gloves on or while walking rows. Audio is generated locally in the browser — nothing is sent anywhere.

mobileNav.title: Mobile bottom navigation
mobileNav.body: On phones the sidebar collapses into five bottom tabs: Status, Zones, Climate, Operate and AI, with a red badge on alerts when advisories are active. The floating green button also opens the AI co-pilot. Plan, Tune and 3D Floor are desktop-oriented views — use a bigger screen for recipe editing.

toasts.title: Toast notifications
toasts.body: The small pop-ups in the corner confirm what just happened — 'Sent', 'Week 6 to HA: 31 written', 'Advisory acknowledged'. They disappear after a couple of seconds and need no action. If a write fails you get a Failed toast instead; re-check the control before assuming it took.

Recipe.title: Active recipe selector
Recipe.body: Picks which saved recipe the whole Plan tab works on — the editor, chart and HA Sync all follow this selection. The label shows the recipe name plus its start date and current grow week once a start date is set. Recipes live in this browser's storage only; switching saves the outgoing recipe first, so nothing is lost.

⇅ HA Sync.title: Push plan to Home Assistant
⇅ HA Sync.body: Opens the sync modal that writes one week of this recipe's crop-steering setpoints to the live engine's number entities. You always see a diff (current HA value versus plan value) and confirm before anything is written; only changed values are sent. It writes irrigation setpoints only — the EC grid, dryback, P0-P3 timing, limits, dripper and zone setup. Climate rows (temp, RH, CO2, PPFD) are plan-only and must be set on the climate controller.

Grow week to apply.title: Week picker and preview
Grow week to apply.body: Choose which week of the recipe to push (week 0 is the first), then Preview & Apply reads every target entity from Home Assistant and lists only what would change, old value to new. It pre-fills from the room's live grow-week sensor so the right week is usually already selected. Nothing writes until you confirm the listed changes. The first time you use it, it asks for your HA long-lived token, which is stored only in this browser.

input_boolean.crop_steering_timeline_aut.title: Timeline autopilot toggle
input_boolean.crop_steering_timeline_aut.body: When ON, and while the Plan tab is open, the dashboard checks the F2 grow-week sensor every minute and automatically pushes the new week's setpoints the moment the grow week ticks over — no manual sync needed at week boundaries. It baselines on the first check, so opening the page never triggers a mass write. Default is OFF; the checkbox is disabled if the autopilot helper entity does not exist in HA. Leave it off unless you trust the recipe end to end.

More actions.title: Recipe overflow menu
More actions.body: The three-dot menu holds recipe management: New recipe, Duplicate (great for an A/B variant of the current run), Rename, Set start date, Compare against another recipe (drawn as a dashed ghost on the chart), Export/Import JSON, Reset to standard plan, and Delete. Delete asks for confirmation and you can never delete the last remaining recipe.

Export JSON.title: Export recipe file
Export JSON.body: Downloads the active recipe as a .grow-recipe.json file — name, start date, phase spans, every per-day setpoint and all zone overrides. Recipes are otherwise stored only in this browser, so export after meaningful edits and to share a recipe between machines or growers.

Import JSON.title: Import recipe file
Import JSON.body: Loads a previously exported recipe file as a brand-new recipe — it never overwrites the one you have open. Older export formats import fine; missing phases or zone overrides just fall back to defaults. After import the new recipe becomes active.

Set start date….title: Grow start date
Set start date….body: Anchors the recipe to the calendar (YYYY-MM-DD). Once set, the recipe bar shows a live 'Week N · Day N' position chip and the chart gains a dashed 'today' marker, so you can see exactly where the crop sits in the plan. Clear it to make the recipe a template again. Note the HA Sync autopilot follows the room's grow-week sensor, not this date.

Reset to standard plan.title: Reset this recipe
Reset to standard plan.body: Wipes the active recipe back to the built-in Athena-style standard plan: default 84-day phase spans, default setpoint curves, and all zone overrides cleared. It only touches the recipe you have open, never your other saved recipes, and asks for confirmation first. There is no undo, so export JSON before resetting if in doubt.

phaseSpine.title: Phase spine — resize phases
phaseSpine.body: The colored blocks are the grow's phases. Tap a block to select it — the editor below then writes to every day of that phase. Drag the seam between two blocks (or use the small minus/plus steppers) to make a phase longer or shorter a day at a time; dragging the final seam changes the total grow length, up to 182 days. Each recipe remembers its own phase spans.

Clone.title: Clone phase (week 0)
Clone.body: Days 1-7 by default: rooting cuttings under 24-hour light, gentle everything — high humidity, low light, feed EC around 2.0-2.5 mS/cm, substrate kept wet near 65% VWC with almost no dryback. Tap the block to edit all clone days at once. Most growers leave this phase close to defaults.

Veg.title: Veg phase (weeks 1-2)
Veg.body: Days 8-21 by default: 18-hour photoperiod, building structure before the flip. Vegetative steering — frequent small irrigations, shallow drybacks (8-12%), moderate EC. Stretch this phase with the seam drag if plants need more size before flower.

Stretch.title: Stretch phase (flower wk 1-3)
Stretch.body: Days 22-42 by default, matching Athena flower weeks 1-3: the flip to 12-hour light and the plant's biggest growth spurt. Light ramps toward 1000 PPFD, feed EC climbs toward 3-4 mS/cm and steering moves generative — deeper drybacks (to about 20%) to set the plant up for flower rather than more leaf.

Bulk Flower.title: Bulk phase (flower wk 4-7)
Bulk Flower.body: Days 43-70 by default, Athena bulk weeks: peak everything — PPFD 1100-1200, CO2 1300-1400 ppm, EC at its highest and the deepest drybacks of the cycle (around 30%). This is the yield-max zone; most of the harvest weight is made here, so hold conditions steady and let the generative steering work.

Late Flower.title: Late flower (finish wk 8)
Late Flower.body: Days 71-77 by default, the Athena finish week: everything backs off — temp drops to about 22 C, RH to mid-50s, PPFD to about 800 and feed EC comes down. Cooler and drier conditions push ripening and suppress botrytis in dense buds. Keep the condensation margin in mind as nights cool.

Flush.title: Flush phase (final week)
Flush.body: Days 78-84 by default: feed EC drops to about 0.8 mS/cm to run residual salts out of the coco, with the room cool (about 20 C) and dry for mould suppression. Drag this phase's end seam to lengthen or shorten the whole grow. The engine's matching live setpoint is the flush EC target in the Steering EC group.

Facility.title: Facility baseline layer
Facility.body: The base layer of the plan — every value you edit here applies to all zones unless a zone has its own override. Think of it as the room recipe; zones inherit it automatically. When pushing to HA, facility values go to the global crop-steering entities.

Zone 1.title: Zone 1 override layer
Zone 1.body: Switch here to give Zone 1 its own values on top of the Facility baseline — only the cells you actually change are stored as overrides; everything else keeps inheriting. The note shows how many parameters and day-cells are overridden, and the clear-overlay chip reverts the whole zone to Facility. On the chart the zone draws solid with the Facility baseline dashed behind it, and HA Sync writes overrides to the zone's own number.crop_steering_zone_1_* entities.

Zone 2.title: Zone 2 override layer
Zone 2.body: Same as the other zone layers: edits here override the Facility baseline for Zone 2 only, and untouched cells keep inheriting. Useful when one row runs a different cultivar or drinks differently. HA Sync writes these overrides to number.crop_steering_zone_2_* entities.

Zone 3.title: Zone 3 override layer
Zone 3.body: Per-zone overlay for Zone 3 — change only what should differ from the room baseline and leave the rest inheriting. The clear-overlay chip wipes all Zone 3 overrides back to Facility after a confirmation. HA Sync writes the overrides to number.crop_steering_zone_3_* entities.

editorScroll.title: Phase editor work surface
editorScroll.body: The main editing pane: every setpoint for the selected phase, grouped by section, with touch-friendly minus/plus steppers. One edit writes that value to every day of the phase on the active layer (Facility or a zone). Everyday groups are open; the advanced groups — Steering EC, Phase Timing, Shot Sizes %, Limits & Hardware, Zone Setup — are collapsed because they are the live engine setpoints that HA Sync writes.

mixed · flatten.title: Mixed value warning
mixed · flatten.body: Appears when a parameter has different values across the days of this phase — usually because you set a per-day ramp or the phase seam moved. The input shows blank with a 'mixed' placeholder plus the min-max range. Tap flatten to write the phase-start value across every day, or open the per-day drill-in to see the ramp.

Per-day.title: Per-day ramp drill-in
Per-day.body: The collapsed strip at the bottom of the editor exposes one cell per day of the selected phase for the parameter currently focused on the chart — use it to ramp a value across a phase (for example stepping EC up through Stretch) instead of one flat number.
