Editing any single day creates a day-level value that overrides the phase. The flatten button puts the whole phase back to one value.

chartHost.title: Focused parameter chart
chartHost.body: Shows ONE parameter across the entire grow as a step line, with phase bands shaded behind it and the selected phase outlined. Pick the parameter with the chips above; click anywhere on the chart to jump to that day's phase. Dashed lines are ghosts: grey for a compare recipe, colored for the Facility baseline when you are on a zone layer, and a cyan 'today' line appears once the recipe has a start date.

Room Temp.title: Day air temperature plan
Room Temp.body: Planned daytime room temperature in C (18-32, 0.5 steps). It is one half of the VPD calculation, so changing it instantly recomputes the derived VPD row. Plan-only: HA Sync never writes climate — set the real room target on your climate controller. Defaults track the Athena curve, around 26-27 C through stretch/bulk and cooling to about 20-22 C at finish.

Leaf Temp.title: Leaf temperature plan
Leaf Temp.body: Planned leaf surface temperature in C — typically 1-2 C below air temp under LEDs. Leaf temp is what actually drives transpiration, so it is the more honest basis for VPD thinking than air temp. Plan-only reference; the live room measures it with the IR leaf sensor on the Climate tab.

RH.title: Relative humidity plan
RH.body: Planned daytime RH in % (30-85). With room temp it sets VPD: too high invites mould and slows transpiration, too low slams stomata shut. Defaults run about 65% through veg and bulk, dropping to the low-to-mid 50s at finish for botrytis safety. Plan-only — drive the real value with the dehumidifiers on Climate.

VPD.title: VPD (auto-computed)
VPD.body: Vapour pressure deficit in kPa, computed automatically from the Room Temp and RH rows — you cannot edit it directly, change temp or RH instead. Roughly 0.8-1.0 in veg, 1.1-1.2 through bulk, 1.2-1.4 at finish. It is the single best one-number check that your temp and RH rows actually make sense together.

Air Velocity.title: Airflow over canopy
Air Velocity.body: Planned air speed across the canopy in m/s (0-2.5). Steady gentle movement (about 0.5-1 m/s at the canopy) keeps boundary-layer humidity from stalling transpiration and discourages mould in dense flower. Plan-only reference for fan setup — there is no automated airflow control.

DLI.title: Daily light integral (auto)
DLI.body: Total light per day in mol/m2/day, computed as PPFD x photoperiod — edit those two rows, not this one. Healthy flower targets sit roughly 35-45 with CO2 enrichment. If a PPFD change makes DLI jump unrealistically, you will see it here before the plants do.

Photoperiod.title: Hours of light per day
Photoperiod.body: Planned light hours (10-24, half-hour steps): defaults are 24 in clone, 18 in veg, 12 from the flip onward. It feeds the DLI calculation and frames the whole irrigation day — the engine's P0-P3 cycle runs lights-on to lights-off. The engine's own schedule comes from the Lights On/Off Hour entities in Limits & Hardware, which HA Sync does write.

VWC Target.title: Substrate moisture target
VWC Target.body: Planned substrate volumetric water content in % (20-80). The default curve steps down generatively through the grow — about 65% in clone, 60% in veg, 50% in stretch, 45% through bulk, drying to 30-40% at the end. Plan-only reference: the engine's live equivalents are P1 Target VWC and the P2 VWC Threshold in the advanced groups.

PW-EC.title: Pore-water EC plan
PW-EC.body: Planned EC of the water held in the substrate, in mS/cm (1-6) — what the roots actually sit in, distinct from feed EC. Generative steering lets pore EC stack above feed EC through dryback. Plan-only sketch; the engine's real targets are the per-phase Steering EC grid, and live readings come from the TEROS pore-EC sensors.

Drain %.title: Planned runoff fraction
Drain %.body: Target daily runoff as % of water applied (0-40). Vegetative steering uses generous drain to keep EC flat; generative bulk often runs low or zero drain so pore EC can stack. Plan-only — the engine does not meter runoff directly, but driving more or fewer P2 shots is what moves real-world drain.

Irrig. Freq..title: Irrigations per day plan
Irrig. Freq..body: Rough planned irrigation events per day (1-16). In the live system shot count is an outcome, not a setting — the engine fires P2 shots whenever VWC crosses the threshold — so treat this row as a sanity check against what Zones reports as actual shots today. Big gaps between plan and actual usually mean shot size or threshold needs tuning.

Plant Steering.title: Veg / Balanced / Generative bias
Plant Steering.body: The day's steering intent as three buttons: Vegetative (frequent wet, shallow dryback — builds leaf and structure), Balanced, or Generative (deeper dryback, higher EC — pushes flower production). The plan defaults run vegetative early and generative from stretch onward. The live engine equivalent is the steering-mode select (system-wide or per zone), which picks which EC and dryback targets apply.

Overnight Dryback.title: Overnight dryback plan
Overnight Dryback.body: Planned overnight dryback in % points dropped from peak VWC (0-50) — always 'dries back BY', never 'dries back to'. Shallow (5-10%) in clone and veg, deepening to about 30% through bulk for generative steering. The engine's live equivalents are the Veg and Gen Dryback Target entities in the Shot Sizes % group, and the P0 Dryback Drop sets how far it must fall after lights-on before ramp-up begins.

P0 Shot (pre-dawn).title: Pre-dawn shot plan
P0 Shot (pre-dawn).body: Planned pre-dawn shot volume in mL per plant (0-500, default 0 early, 50-100 mL mid-grow). Plan-only reference from the original feed chart: the live engine treats P0 as a no-irrigation dryback window after lights-on, so it never fires planned P0 shots. Useful when running the room manually or comparing against hand-feed schedules.

P1 Shot (wake-up).title: Ramp-up shot plan
P1 Shot (wake-up).body: Planned size of the morning ramp-up shots in mL per plant (0-500). The live engine sizes P1 shots as a % of substrate volume instead — starting at the P1 Initial Shot and growing by the increment each shot until the P1 Target VWC is reached (those live setpoints are in the Shot Sizes % group). Use this row to sanity-check that the plan's mL roughly matches the engine's % maths for your pot size.

P2 Shots (day).title: Maintenance shot plan
P2 Shots (day).body: Planned size of each daytime maintenance shot in mL per plant (0-400). The live engine fires a P2 shot of P2 Shot Size % whenever VWC drops below the P2 VWC Threshold, with the threshold nudged up or down by the EC ratio (water more when EC runs high, less when low). Defaults run 60-120 mL across the grow.

P3 Shot (end).title: End-of-day shot plan
P3 Shot (end).body: Planned final shot of the day in mL per plant (0-400), setting the starting VWC for the overnight dryback. In the live engine P3 is emergency-only: it fires a rescue shot only if VWC falls below the P3 Emergency threshold overnight, and the veg/gen 'last irrigation' timers decide how long before lights-off planned watering stops.

Tank pH.title: Feed pH plan
Tank pH.body: Planned pH of the feed tank (5.0-7.0, defaults 5.8-6.1 following Athena). The engine guards this live: the Irrig pH Min/Max gates in Limits & Hardware block all irrigation when source-water pH drifts outside the band, so a drifting doser stops the room rather than feeding bad water.

Tank EC (feed).title: Feed EC plan
Tank EC (feed).body: Planned input EC of the feed in mS/cm (0.5-5.0): about 2.5 in clone, 3.0 from veg through bulk, tapering to 2.0-2.5 at finish per Athena. This is what you mix in the tank; pore EC in the blocks stacks higher through dryback. The live Irrig EC Min/Max gates block irrigation if the source water reads outside the allowed band.

Steering EC.title: Steering EC grid (advanced)
Steering EC.body: The per-phase EC targets the engine actually runs on: a vegetative row and a generative row for each of P0-P3, plus the flush target, in mS/cm. The active target is picked by the zone's current phase and steering mode, and the live EC ratio (current divided by target) drives the P2 threshold adjustment. HA Sync writes these to number.crop_steering_ec_target_* — this group, not the Tank EC row, is what changes irrigation behaviour.

Phase Timing.title: Phase timing setpoints (advanced)
Phase Timing.body: The clocks of the daily cycle: P0 min/max wait (earliest and latest the morning dryback can end), P1 time between shots and min/max shot counts, and the P3 veg/gen last-irrigation cutoffs before lights-off. These control when the engine moves between phases rather than how much it waters. HA Sync writes them to the matching number.crop_steering_* entities.

Shot Sizes %.title: Shot size setpoints (advanced)
Shot Sizes %.body: The core irrigation maths, all as % of substrate volume: P1 target VWC, initial/increment/max ramp shot sizes, the P2 threshold and shot size with its EC high/low ratio adjusters, the P0 dryback drop that ends the morning wait, the P3 emergency floor and rescue shot, and the veg/gen overnight dryback targets. These are the numbers that most change how the room waters — adjust one thing at a time and watch a full day before the next change. HA Sync writes them live.

Limits & Hardware.title: Safety limits and hardware (advanced)
Limits & Hardware.body: The guard rails and plumbing constants: field capacity (VWC above which all irrigation is blocked), substrate volume and dripper flow/count (which convert shot % into valve seconds), lights on/off hours (the engine's day boundaries), the maximum substrate EC cutoff, the source-water EC and pH gates, and the blocked-dripper trip count. Get substrate volume and dripper flow right or every shot in the room is mis-sized. HA Sync writes these to the global entities.

Zone Setup.title: Per-zone setup (advanced)
Zone Setup.body: The three per-zone-only numbers: plant count (scales total volume), max daily volume in litres (a hard daily cap per zone — emergency rescues are exempt), and the shot size multiplier (0.1-5x, a per-row trim for a weak or thirsty row). These have no global version; HA Sync always writes them to each zone's number.crop_steering_zone_N_* entities using that zone's layer values.
